_comment: Timeline moment markers for the July 2 EVENING phase (day-expansion slice 2). Times are phase battle-clock seconds (t=0 = 19:29 LMT sunset, startTime 70140; 22:30 = 10860). Every marker cites the anchor or editorial decision that places it (ED-29/ED-62 chain; ED-61; ED-63). The `battle` field gates this file to its own phase.

moments.label: Sunset
moments.detail: 19:29 local mean time. Johnson's three brigades are on Culp's Hill's east face; the XII Corps — ordered away at 6:30 — is on the Baltimore Pike; Greene holds the works alone.
moments.citation: ED-31 astronomical pin; CA-J2E-1 (or-27-1-greene, PRIMARY basis per ED-62)

moments.label: Hays and Avery step off
moments.detail: East Cemetery Hill, 19:45–20:00 — the three-source both-armies agreement (Hays 'a little before 8', Wiedrich 'about 8', Ricketts 'at about 8'), triggered by Johnson's engagement per Early's own order record.

moments.label: The lodgments
moments.detail: Hays's and Avery's men among Wiedrich's and Ricketts's guns — possession momentary, no gun leaves the hill; on Culp's Hill, Steuart occupies the works the XII Corps vacated, and that lodgment holds overnight.
moments.citation: ED-63 (the convergent-event ruling); the Steuart works record (or-27-2-steuart; ED-57-class occupation)

moments.label: The hill is cleared
moments.detail: Carroll's brigade and the rallied XI Corps regiments retake the batteries (~20:30 — a sequence anchor; no hard clock exists on either side). Avery lies mortally wounded on the ascent; both batteries resume service the same night.

moments.label: The guns come off the field
moments.detail: By ~21:00 the eight abandoned guns of Bigelow's 9th Massachusetts and Watson's I/5th US are hauled to the rear — one loss and one recovery ledger per battery, never per-claimant duplicates. Ruger's returning column reaches Rock Creek ('9. P.M.' in Bachelder's own hand).
moments.citation: ED-61 (or-27-1-mcgilvery pp. 881-884; Milton No. 320); bachelder j2-05 drawn annotation

moments.label: The fighting dies down
moments.detail: About 10 o'clock — Hays back at his jump-off field, Greene's front quiet, Kane's returning brigade fired on from its own vacated works. Nicholls's reading runs to ~23:00 and is recorded, not adopted.
